Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. More precise elevation data for GraphHopper routing engine 54 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. Two studies in compiler optimisations 86 upvotes, 8 comments
  3. Quantization from the Ground Up 267 upvotes, 50 comments
  4. The Cassandra of 'The Machine' 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. The EU still wants to scan your private messages and photos 1227 upvotes, 330 comments
  6. Personal Encyclopedias 247 upvotes, 59 comments
  7. Obsolete Sounds 41 upvotes, 6 comments
  8. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 653 upvotes, 209 comments
  9. The Last Contract: William T. Vollmann's Battle to Publish an Epic (2025) 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  10. My DIY FPGA board can run Quake II 164 upvotes, 51 comments
  11. LibreOffice and the Art of Overreacting 21 upvotes, 3 comments
  12. Thoughts on slowing the fuck down 886 upvotes, 395 comments
  13. False claims in a widely-cited paper 296 upvotes, 121 comments
  14. Swift 6.3 94 upvotes, 33 comments
  15. Earthquake scientists reveal how overplowing weakens soil at experimental farm 168 upvotes, 79 comments
  16. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 128 upvotes, 60 comments
  17. Government agencies buy commercial data about Americans in bulk 73 upvotes, 32 comments
  18. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 19 upvotes, 5 comments
  19. Apple randomly closes bug reports unless you "verify" the bug remains unfixed 413 upvotes, 242 comments
  20. 90% of Claude-linked output going to GitHub repos w <2 stars 300 upvotes, 184 comments
  21. ARC-AGI-3 415 upvotes, 264 comments
  22. The truth that haunts the Ramones: 'They sold more T-shirts than records' 142 upvotes, 84 comments
  23. Niche Museums 15 upvotes, 5 comments
  24. Show HN: Optio – Orchestrate AI coding agents in K8s to go from ticket to PR 55 upvotes, 33 comments
  25. Supreme Court Sides with Cox in Copyright Fight over Pirated Music 354 upvotes, 279 comments
  26. "Disregard That" Attacks 89 upvotes, 62 comments
  27. Maxell MXCP-P100 – wireless cassette player 30 upvotes, 17 comments
  28. Show HN: Robust LLM Extractor for Websites in TypeScript 47 upvotes, 33 comments
  29. What came after the 486? 46 upvotes, 40 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments
